__data__.text:
To be "job-ready" in 2026, you must move beyond simple "Chat with PDF" tutorials. Employers are now looking for developers who can build Agentic Workflows—systems that don't just talk, but actually execute tasks using tools, manage complex state, and self-correct.

Here is the essential tech stack you need to master, categorized by their role in the "Agentic Lifecycle."



1. Orchestration & Multi-Agent Frameworks

These are the "brains" of your application. They manage how agents talk to each other and how they decide which tools to use.

LangGraph (Top Priority): The industry standard for stateful agents. Unlike basic LangChain, LangGraph allows for "loops" (cyclic graphs), which are necessary for an agent to try a task, fail, and try again.

CrewAI: The best for role-based multi-agent systems. You define a "Researcher" and a "Writer," and CrewAI handles the hand-offs between them.

Microsoft AutoGen: A powerful framework for event-driven multi-agent conversations. It’s highly scalable and favored in enterprise environments.



2. Data Connection & Retrieval (RAG)

Agents are useless if they don't have access to your data. You need to know how to index and retrieve information efficiently.

LlamaIndex: While LangChain is great for logic, LlamaIndex is the king of data. Learn it for advanced RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) and connecting to complex data sources (SQL, APIs, Slack, Google Drive).

Vector Databases: You must know at least one "Managed" and one "Local" database:

Pinecone or Weaviate (Managed/Cloud)

ChromaDB or Qdrant (Local/Open Source)

pgvector: (PostgreSQL) Essential for companies that want to keep their AI data in their existing SQL databases.

3. Reliability & "Agentic" Infrastructure

This is what separates a hobbyist from a professional.

Pydantic: Non-negotiable. You use this to force the LLM to output structured data (JSON) that your Python code can actually process.

LiteLLM: A "Universal I/O" library. It allows you to call 100+ different LLMs (OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, Ollama) using the exact same code format.

DSPy: A newer, high-level framework that focuses on programming rather than "prompting." It automatically optimizes your prompts and weights, which is a massive trend for 2026.



4. Observability & Evaluation (The "Job-Ready" Edge)

In a professional setting, you must prove your agent works. You can't just "vibe check" the answers.

LangSmith / Langfuse: These are "Tracer" tools. They allow you to see exactly where an agent failed in a 10-step process and how much each step cost.

Arize Phoenix / DeepEval: Frameworks for Unit Testing your AI. They use "LLM-as-a-Judge" to grade your agent's answers on faithfulness, relevancy, and hallucination.



Summary Checklist for your Resume:

Category

Must-Learn Tools

Logic/Agents

LangGraph, CrewAI, AutoGen

Data/RAG

LlamaIndex, Pinecone, pgvector

Developer Experience

Pydantic, LiteLLM, DSPy

Monitoring

LangSmith, Langfuse, Arize Phoenix

Deployment

FastAPI, Docker, Ollama (for local testing)

The "Golden Rule" for 2026:

Don't just learn the syntax; learn the design patterns. Practice building a system where Agent A writes code, Agent B tries to run it, and if it fails, Agent B sends the error back to Agent A to fix it. That "loop" is the heart of Agentic AI.



1. The "Autonomous Researcher" (Multi-Agent System)

Instead of a basic RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) bot, build a team of specialized agents that work together to produce a comprehensive report.

The Concept: A user provides a complex topic (e.g., "The impact of 2026 semiconductor regulations on EV pricing").

The Architecture:

Agent A (Researcher): Searches the web and extracts data.

Agent B (Analyst): Critiques the data for bias and summarizes key points.

Agent C (Writer): Formats the findings into a professional PDF.

Tech Stack: CrewAI or Microsoft AutoGen, OpenAI/Claude API, Serper API (for search).

Resume Keyphrase: "Orchestrated multi-agent hierarchical workflows to reduce hallucination rates by X%."

2. Self-Correcting SQL Agent

Standard text-to-SQL often fails on complex queries. Build an agent that doesn't just write code, but tests and fixes it.

The Concept: An interface where non-technical users ask questions about a database.

The Agentic Loop: The agent writes a SQL query $\rightarrow$ Executes it against a dummy DB $\rightarrow$ If it hits an error, the agent reads the error message and rewrites the query until it works.

Tech Stack: LangGraph (for the cyclic graph logic), SQLAlchemy, SQLite, Groq (for fast inference).

Resume Keyphrase: "Implemented an iterative 'Reflection' pattern for SQL generation, achieving 90% accuracy on complex join queries."

3. Multimodal Personal Assistant with "Memory"

Most AI agents forget who you are the moment you refresh. This project focuses on Long-Term Memory and multimodal input.

The Concept: A voice/text assistant that can "see" images you upload (like a receipt or a workout plan) and remembers your preferences over time.

The Innovation: Use a Vector Database to store past interactions and a "summarization" agent that updates a "User Profile" document after every session.

Tech Stack: Pinecone or Weaviate (Vector DB), GPT-4o (Multimodal), Whisper (Speech-to-Text), Streamlit.

Resume Keyphrase: "Developed a persistent memory architecture using vector embeddings to maintain user context across sessions."

4. Automated Code Reviewer & Unit Test Generator

Targeting the "Agentic AI for Software Engineering" niche.

The Concept: A GitHub Action that triggers whenever code is pushed. The agent reviews the code for security flaws, suggests optimizations, and automatically writes the unit tests for the new functions.

The Logic: The agent must parse the AST (Abstract Syntax Tree) of the code to understand the logic before suggesting changes.

Tech Stack: LangChain, GitHub API, Python/JavaScript, Llama 3 (running locally via Ollama).

Resume Keyphrase: "Built a CI/CD integrated Agentic pipeline that automated 40% of routine code review tasks."



Pro-Tips for your Resume:

Focus on Evaluation: Don't just say you built it. Mention how you evaluated it (e.g., using RAGAS for RAG metrics or DeepEval).

Quantify Efficiency: Use phrases like "Reduced token consumption by 30% through prompt compression" or "Decreased latency by using a local SLM (Small Language Model) for routing."

Tool Use: Emphasize that your agents "use tools" (APIs, Calculators, Python Interpreters) rather than just "chatting."

The examination will be conducted online. You will be required to enter Login ID and Password, as given above, to enter theOn-Line exam site. Please ensure that your Name and other details which will subsequently appear on the screen are correct.You must maintain secrecy of the username & password.
Please note that your name as appearing on the call letter (provided by you during the process of registration) shouldexactly match the name as appearing on the photo identity proof. Female candidates who have changedfirst/last/middle name post marriage must take special note of this. If there is any mismatch between the nameindicated in the Call Letter and Photo Identity Proof you will not be allowed to appear for the exam.In case ofcandidates who have changed their name will be allowed only if they produce Gazette notification/their marriagecertificate/affidavit.
A handout giving information/instructions about the type of examination is available on the website of PFRDA. Pleasedownload the handout and study it carefully.
Candidates should put their Left Thumb Impression clearly and sign in the respective space provided on the call-letter in thepresence of the invigilator.
You must bring a ball point pen and an ink stamp pad with you.
The mere fact that the call letter has been issued to you does not imply that your candidature has been finally cleared byPFRDA or that the entries made by you in your application for examination have been accepted by PFRDA as true and correct.It may be noted that PFRDA will take up verification of eligible candidates, i.e., age, educational qualifications and categorywith reference to original documents, only at the time of interview of candidates who qualify on the basis of result of the onlineexamination.

You may therefore please note that if at any stage, it is found that, you do not satisfy the eligibility criteria as
given in the advertisement or the information furnished by you in the application is incorrect, your candidature is liable to becancelled forth-with with such further actions as PFRDA may like to take.
You are called for the on-line examination without verifying your personal details viz age, qualification etc. with yourcertificates. Before appearing for the on-line examination, you must ensure that you fulfill the eligibility criteria stipulated by thePFRDA in all respects.
Your responses (answers) will be analysed with other candidates to detect patterns of similarity of right and wrong
answers. If in the analytical procedure adopted in this regard, it is inferred/concluded that the responses have beenshared and scores obtained are not genuine/valid, your candidature may be cancelled and/or the result withheld.
You must report at the examination venue 15 minutes before the time as printed on this call-letter. Candidates arrivinglate will not be permitted to appear for the on-line examination.
Use of books, notebooks, calculators, watch calculators, pagers, mobile phonesetc. is not permitted in this examination.
Candidates are advised not to bring any of the banned items including mobile phones/pagers to the venue of examination assafety arrangement cannot be assured. Any candidate found resorting to any unfair means or malpractice or any misconductwhile appearing for the examination including giving/receiving help to/from any candidate during the examination will bedisqualified. The candidate should be vigilant to ensure that no other candidate is able to copy from his/her answers.
Any request for change of post/date/session/centre/venue will not be entertained.
You may please note that this call-letter does not constitute an offer of employment with PFRDA.
Candidates should bear their own travelling and other expenses to attend online examination and the candidates have tomake their own arrangements for boarding/lodging.
Any canvassing by or on behalf of the candidates or to bring any influence with regard to their selection/recruitment shall beconsidered as DISQUALIFICATION.
The possibility of occurrence of some problem in the administration of the examination cannot be ruled out completely which
may impact test delivery and/or result from being generated.

In that event, every effort will be made to rectify such problem,which may include movement of candidates, delay in test. Conduct of a re-exam is at the absolute discretion of test conductingbody. Candidates will not have any claim for a re-test. Candidates not willing to move or not willing to participate in the delayedprocess of test delivery shall be summarily rejected from the process.
If the examination is held in more than one session, the scores across various sessions will be equated to adjust for slight
differences in difficulty level of different test batteries used across sessions. More than one session are required if the nodescapacity is less or some technical disruption takes place at any centre or for any candidate.
Instances for providing incorrect information and/or process violation by a candidate detected at any stage of the selection,process will lead to disqualification of the candidate from the selection process and he/she will not be allowed to appear in anyrecruitment process of the PFRDA in the future. If such instances go undetected during the current selection process but are
detected subsequently, such disqualification will take place with retrospective effect.
Anyone found to be disclosing, publishing reproducing, transmitting, storing or facilitating transmission and storage of testcontents in any form or any information therein in whole or part thereof or by any means verbal or written, electronic ormechanical or taking away the papers supplied in the examination hall or found to be in unauthorised possession of testcontent is likely to be prosecuted.
Persons with Benchmark Disabilities (PwBD) should contact the Test Centre Administrator of the test venue at least 30
minutes before the time of examination for assistance in seating, if needed.
Candidates are advised to take note of “The Public Examinations (Prevention of Unfair Means) Act, 2024”.
